tech_news.title: The end of the ISS is looming, and the US could have a big problem - CNN
tech_news.description: As NASA aims to return astronauts to the moon, a multibillion-dollar question mark looms: What will the space agency do when the International Space Station retires?

tech_news.title: iOS 26.3.1 (a): Apple’s Hidden iPhone Update Recommended For Hundreds Of Millions Of Users - Forbes
tech_news.description: The latest iPhone update has arrived and it’s different from previous updates. Here’s why and what to do about it.

tech_news.title: iOS 26.4 will automatically turn on strict security feature for all iPhones - 9to5Mac
tech_news.description: iOS 26.4 is the next big iPhone release, and Apple plans to enable a key security feature by default in the update: Stolen Device Protection.

tech_news.title: Apple explains why M5 chips have three different core types in new interview - 9to5Mac
tech_news.description: Apple explains M5's three core types: super cores for single-thread tasks, performance cores for multi-threading, and efficiency cores.

tech_news.title: Gamers Hate Nvidia's DLSS 5. Developers Aren’t Crazy About It, Either - WIRED
tech_news.description: Nvidia’s new AI upscaling gaming technology struck gamers as uncanny and off-putting. Developers don't seem to like it, either, but it could be “the default” in a few years.

tech_news.title: Samsung's Galaxy Z TriFold Replaced My Phone, Tablet, and Laptop—But There's a Big Catch - PCMag
tech_news.description: The Galaxy Z TriFold aims to be an all-in-one device—serving as your phone, tablet, and even a makeshift laptop. It largely delivers on that promise, though its price is undeniably steep, and Samsung is no longer selling new models.

tech_news.title: WordPress.com now lets AI agents write and publish posts, and more - TechCrunch
tech_news.description: New AI agents on WordPress.com could lower barriers to publishing while increasing machine-generated content across the web.
